description: Implement FGAP-140: no install/update/status path seeds a fresh substrate's migrations.json with the block-schema migration declarations the catalog already carries (samples/migrations.json has ready-made chains for all 18 schemas; seedCatalogConfigMigrationDecls seeds config only). First step, per the gap's own proposed_resolution: determine the class-correct mechanism -- installContext fresh-install seeding versus generalizing check-status's config-only seeding to all schema kinds -- via derive-decisions-from-facts against the existing seeding precedents (seedCatalogConfigMigrationDecls, registerCatalogMigrationChainIfKnown) and file the decision; then implement the decided mechanism so a substrate's migration history is complete from birth.
acceptance_criteria: The install-time-vs-status-time mechanism choice is filed as a decision (derived per derive-decisions-from-facts, or escalated if genuinely underdetermined) before implementation; the implementation matches it

A fresh /context install against the current catalog yields context-validate clean for session-notes with no manual write-schema-migration step -- the schema=1.1.0 / starter schema_version=1.0.0 bridge exists from birth, verified by a real fresh-install runtime demo
The seeding covers every freshly-installed schema with a catalog migration chain, not session-notes alone -- the general class, with a unit pin
Existing seedCatalogConfigMigrationDecls (config) and registerCatalogMigrationChainIfKnown (update-time) behavior unchanged (regression)
build, check, and full test suite exit 0; docs-surface-sync applied to any changed op description or README statement about install/status behavior
